title: marsilea farinosa subsp. arrecta
intro: Marsilea farinosa subsp. arrecta is a species of fern, commonly known as the "pepperwort" or "four-leaf clover," characterized by its unique four-leaf arrangement and ability to thrive in aquatic environments. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been utilized to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Marsilea farinosa subsp. arrecta include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and act as a natural diuretic. Herbal preparations of the plant often involve infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
